title: IDF Rehabilitation Department Reform: New Support for Veterans
summary: The public committee for the IDF Rehabilitation Department reform recommends turning the unit into an independent authority with enhanced mental health support.
content:
A public committee in Israel recently submitted comprehensive recommendations for a major IDF Rehabilitation Department reform to improve veteran care. The proposed IDF Rehabilitation Department reform seeks to transform the department into an independent authority, ensuring every wounded soldier receives a personal companion throughout their recovery. This initiative aims to eliminate bureaucratic obstacles and provide immediate, personalized medical and psychological assistance.

A significant portion of the recommendations focuses on expanding resources for PTSD and mental health resilience, aligning with the mission of Genesis Friends For Life. By prioritizing holistic care and integrated therapy, the plan strengthens national resilience and provides a robust social safety net for those affected by combat trauma.
